text: The surgeon resects the affected segment of the colon, following the procedural requirements for a colectomy.
The surgeon resects the mesentery of the proximal and distal colon segments, using indocyanine green (ICG) fluorescence to mark the section area.
The surgeon removes the anvil from the back and introduces it through a colotomy into the healthy colon near the knot area, preparing for anastomosis.
The surgeon uses a six-tip blue surform to section the affected segment of the colon at both the proximal and distal ends.
The surgeon places the resected segment of the colon into a surgical bag for later extraction from the abdominal cavity.
The surgeon extracts the anvil through the terminal part of the proximal colon and creates a reinforced pull-string suture in the distal part of the colon.
The surgeon extracts the proximal part of the anvil through the forearm under visual control, completing the boosting phase of the procedure.
The surgeon performs a mechanical end-to-end anastomosis, connecting the two ends of the colon after resection.
